title: High-Quality Extraction of Medium-to-Long DNA Fragments from Biogas Digestate for Nanopore Sequencing
author: He Sun

steps.title: Decant the beads from the PowerBead...
steps.instruction: Decant the beads from the PowerBead Pro Tube into a new 1.5 mL Microcentrifuge Tube.

steps.title: Using a cut 1000 μl pipette...
steps.instruction: Using a cut 1000 μl pipette tip, transfer 600 μl + 600 μl of homogenized digestate sample into the empty PowerBead Pro Tube. 1200 µL, Digestate Sample

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 10,000 × g for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 10,000 × g for 5 minutes. 10000 x g, 20 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Weigh the empty PowerBead Pro Tube and record the weight
steps.instruction: Weigh the empty PowerBead Pro Tube and record the weight.

steps.title: Carefully decant the supernatant, weigh the...
steps.instruction: Carefully decant the supernatant, weigh the PowerBead Pro Tube again, and record the weight. Note : Calculate the sample weight.

steps.title: Decant the beads back into the PowerBead Pro Tube
steps.instruction: Decant the beads back into the PowerBead Pro Tube.

steps.title: Add 800 μl of Solution CD1
steps.instruction: Add 800 μl of Solution CD1. Vortex briefly to mix. 800 µL, Solution CD1

steps.title: Homogenize via FastPrep‱-24 Classic I at...
steps.instruction: Homogenize via FastPrep‱-24 Classic I at 6 m/s for 20 s. Immediately place the tube on ice after lysis. 00:00:20 The homogenization parameters may be adjusted according to sample type.

steps.title: Centrifuge the PowerBead Pro Tube at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the PowerBead Pro Tube at 15,000 x g for 2 min. 15000 x g, 20 °C, 2 minutes

steps.title: Carefully decant the supernatant into a new 2
steps.instruction: Carefully decant the supernatant into a new 2.0 mL Microcentrifuge Tube.

steps.title: Repeat step 9
steps.instruction: Repeat step 9.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a clean 2 mL Microcentrifuge Tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a clean 2 mL Microcentrifuge Tube. Note: Expect 500- 600 μl . The supernatant may still contain some soil particles.

steps.title: Add 200 μl of Solution CD2...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl of Solution CD2 to the tube and mix thoroughly by gently inverting for 1 min. 200 µL, CD2 00:01:00

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15,000 x g for 90 s
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15,000 x g for 90 s. Carefully transfer up to 700 µL of the supernatant into a clean 2.0 mL Microcentrifuge Tube, avoiding disturbance of the pellet . Note: Expect 500- 600 μl. 15000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute 30 seconds

steps.title: Repeat step 12, centrifuging for 60 s instead. Note
steps.instruction: Repeat step 12, centrifuging for 60 s instead. Note: Almost all of the liquid can be transferred. A pellet mark may be visible on the tube wall. 15000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Add 600 μl of Solution CD3...
steps.instruction: Add 600 μl of Solution CD3 and mix thoroughly by gently inverting for 1 min. 600 µL, CD3 00:01:00 If DNA purification is planned, bring the magnetic beads to equilibrate to room temperature at this step.

steps.title: Load 650 μl of lysate to an MB Spin Column
steps.instruction: Load 650 μl of lysate to an MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 1 min. 8000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and repeat step...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and repeat step 14 to ensure that all of the lysate has passed through the MB Spin Column. 8000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Carefully place the MB Spin Column...
steps.instruction: Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a clean 2 mL Collection Tube. Avoid splashing any flow-through onto the MB Spin Column.

steps.title: Add 500 μl of Solution EA...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl of Solution EA to the MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 1 min. 500 µL, Solution EA 8000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and place the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column back into the same 2 mL Collection Tube.

steps.title: Add 500 μl of Solution C5...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl of Solution C5 to the MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 1 min. 500 µL, Solution C5 8000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute

steps.title: Discard the flow-through and place the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column into a NEW 2 ml Collection Tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at up to 15,000 x g for 2 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at up to 15,000 x g for 2 min. Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a new 2.0 mL Microcentrifuge Tube. 15000 x g, 20 °C, 2 minutes

steps.title: Add 62 μl of Solution C6...
steps.instruction: Add 62 μl of Solution C6 to the center of the white filter membrane. Incubate for 5 min. 62 µL, Solution C6 00:05:00

steps.title: Centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 3 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 3 min. Discard the MB Spin Column. The DNA is now ready for downstream applications. 8000 x g, 20 °C, 3 minutes Use 1.5 μl of the eluate to assess the DNA quality and estimate concentration via Nanodrop.

steps.title: Add 21 µL of magnetic beads (0
steps.instruction: Add 21 µL of magnetic beads (0.35× volume; e.g., AMPure or HighPrep) to 60 µL of DNA sample. Mix thoroughly by gentle hand shaking and flicking until the beads are evenly suspended. 60 µL, DNA Sample 21 µL, Magnetic Beads Note : Bring the beads to room temperature for at least 30 min before use.

steps.title: Incubate the mixture at room temperature for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate the mixture at room temperature for 10 minutes. 00:10:00

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the tube, then place...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the tube, then place it on a magnetic stand until the solution becomes clear and the beads are fully bound.

steps.title: While the tube remains on the...
steps.instruction: While the tube remains on the magnet, carefully remove and discard the supernatant without disturbing the bead pellet.

steps.title: (wash 1/2 ) Add 200 µL...
steps.instruction: (wash 1/2 ) Add 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol. Avoid disturbing the beads. Incubate for 30s. 200 µL, Fresh 80% Ethanol 00:00:30

steps.title: (wash 1/2 ) Remove the supernatant
steps.instruction: (wash 1/2 ) Remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat steps 28-29
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 28-29. If the final DNA purity is insufficient, perform up to three wash steps. Note that excessive washing may lead to increased DNA loss.

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the tube, place it...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the tube, place it on the magnetic stand, and remove any residual liquid with a 10 µL pipette.

steps.title: Air-dry the beads for 1–2 minutes,...
steps.instruction: Air-dry the beads for 1–2 minutes, avoiding over-drying or cracking of the pellet.

steps.title: Add 30 µL of nuclease-free water...
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL of nuclease-free water and resuspend the beads gently by shaking and flicking until fully dispersed. 30 µL, Nuclease-free water

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 10 minutes. 00:10:00 37 °C

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the tube, then place...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the tube, then place it on the magnetic stand until the solution is clear and the beads are fully bound.

steps.title: Collect the supernatant, which contains the purified DNA
steps.instruction: Collect the supernatant, which contains the purified DNA. 30 µL, DNA Eluate Use 1.5 µL of the eluate to assess DNA purity with a NanoDrop spectrophotometer. Use 2 µL to determine DNA concentration with a Qubit fluorometer. Use 5 µL to evaluate DNA fragment length by agarose gel electrophoresis.

steps.title: For short-term storage, keep the extracted...
steps.instruction: For short-term storage, keep the extracted DNA at 4 °C and proceed with sequencing library preparation as soon as possible.
